description: Psychiatry progress note — major depressive disorder follow-up, medication adjustment, PHQ-9 scored
parsed_text:
PSYCHIATRY PROGRESS NOTE
Harbor Behavioral Health Center
Department: Outpatient Psychiatry
Encounter ID: HBH-PSY-2025-020447
Date: 2025-11-05

Provider: Dr. Asha Mehta, MD
NPI: 6677889900
Specialty: Psychiatry

Patient: Brennan, Christopher J.
DOB: 1989-12-02
MRN: HBH-MRN-0055891

Facility: Harbor Behavioral Health Center
Department: Outpatient Psychiatry

---
CHIEF COMPLAINT
Follow-up for depression management. Patient reports partial improvement on current medication.

SUBJECTIVE
35-year-old male presenting for follow-up of major depressive disorder (MDD). Started sertraline 50 mg 6 weeks ago. Reports some improvement in mood and motivation, but still experiencing significant fatigue, hypersomnia (sleeping 10-12 hours), and poor concentration affecting work performance. PHQ-9 score today: 14 (moderate depression). Previous PHQ-9 was 19 (severe) at initial visit. No suicidal ideation, homicidal ideation, or psychotic symptoms. Denies alcohol or substance use. Therapist sessions ongoing, weekly with Dr. Lau.

OBJECTIVE
Vitals:
  BP: 122/78 mmHg   HR: 72 bpm   Temp: 98.0°F   RR: 14 breaths/min
  SpO2: 99% on room air   Weight: 178 lbs

Mental Status Exam:
  Appearance: Well-groomed, appropriate dress. Behavior: Cooperative, mild psychomotor slowing.
  Speech: Normal rate and volume. Mood: "A little better but still dragging." Affect: Mildly dysphoric, restricted range.
  Thought process: Linear, goal-directed. Thought content: No SI/HI, no delusions, no AH/VH.
  Cognition: Alert, oriented x4. Insight: Good. Judgment: Intact.

ASSESSMENT
1. Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1) — Partial response to sertraline 50 mg at 6 weeks. PHQ-9 improved from 19 to 14 but still symptomatic with residual fatigue and hypersomnia.
2. Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1) — Subthreshold symptoms, not primary complaint today, monitoring.

PLAN
1. Increase sertraline to 100 mg daily (titration per MDD protocol). Counseled on expected delay of 2-4 weeks for full effect.
2. Continue weekly therapy with Dr. Lau. Recommend adding CBT focus on behavioral activation.
3. Sleep hygiene education provided — limit sleep to 8 hours, consistent wake time, avoid daytime napping.
4. PHQ-9 to be repeated at next visit.
5. Patient instructed to call crisis line (988) or go to nearest ED if suicidal ideation develops.
6. Follow-up in 4 weeks to reassess sertraline dose response.

Electronically signed: Dr. Asha Mehta, MD — 2025-11-05 10:58
